allocations.rationale: Arbitrage (equity-taxed) avoids slab tax on short-term gains.
Aggressive hybrid (equity-taxed) outperforms debt funds for 6-year goals.
Index fund (equity-taxed) offers passive growth with minimal fees.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera. Step 2: Start ₹8,000/month SIPs in the above funds. Step 3: Increase SIP by ₹500 every 6 months to build corpus.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm SIPs are active.
After 1 year: Check if any fund underperforms its category.
After 5 years: Rebalance to maintain 20%/30%/50% allocation.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, adjust allocations if any fund grows more than 10% above its target. Move excess to underperforming funds.
plan.rationale: With 30 years ahead, equity funds (index and aggressive hybrid) will grow faster than debt. The 20% in arbitrage covers liquidity needs without slab tax. This mix balances growth and safety for your goals.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in bad years, but your portfolio will recover over time.
The emergency fund in arbitrage ensures short-term stability.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

aps.reasoning: Passive Instrument Fraction: 60% of equity allocation (50% index, 20% arbitrage) is passive. Turnover Score: Annual rebalancing and 6-month review suggest moderate turnover. Cost Emphasis: Low expense ratios and focus on minimal fees. Research vs Cost: More cost-focused than research-driven. Time Horizon: Long-term with 30-year perspective. Portfolio Activeness: Index fund is passive, arbitrage and hybrid show some active traits but overall portfolio is a mix.

pqs.reasoning: Goal alignment: The plan addresses emergency fund, marriage savings, and long-term wealth with equity-focused allocations. Diversification: Limited to 3 funds across 2 categories (arbitrage, aggressive hybrid, index), lacks sector and geographic diversification. Risk-return: Moderate risk with 30-year horizon, projected returns are reasonable. Internal consistency: Rationale and allocations align, rebalancing and risk warnings are coherent. Tax efficiency: Arbitrage and aggressive hybrid are equity-taxed, suitable for new regime; index fund is also equity-taxed, which is optimal for new regime investors.
